title: Arthrophytum regelii
taxonomy: Arthrophytum regelii is classified within the plant family Asclepiadaceae, which has since been revised and merged into the family Apocynaceae. It is also known as 'Russian thistle' or 'Prickly saltwort', although it is not a true thistle. The plant is native to Central Asia but has been naturalized in other parts of the world. It belongs to the genus Arthrophytum and is a succulent shrub that grows up to 1 meter in height. The classification of Arthrophytum regelii may continue to be refined as botanical knowledge evolves.
